plainEnglish: This is the Level AAA version of 1.4.5, with no 'customizable' exception: images of text are only allowed for pure decoration or when a specific presentation is truly essential (logos count as essential). In practice, render all meaningful text as real text.
whyItMatters: Low-vision and screen reader users can't resize, recolor, or read any meaningful text that's delivered as an image rather than as real text.
quickCheck: Try to select the text in any image-like element; meaningful text that can't be selected should be real text, with images reserved for logos and decoration.
commonMistakes: Using images of text for headings or pull-quotes to get a specific font, instead of loading that font as a web font.
Rasterizing styled text into an image because a designer handed off a PNG, rather than reproducing it with CSS.

codeExamples.label: Quote rendered as real, styled text
Quote delivered as an image of text
